# Template Configuration

A complete reference for all fields in a template spec. Pass the spec as the `spec` field when creating or updating a template.

## Full Spec Example

```yaml
spec:
    displayName: "Python 3.12 Data Science"
    description: "Python with numpy, pandas, and jupyter pre-installed"
    tags:
        - python
        - data-science

    mainContainer:
        image: registry.sandbox0.io/my-ds-env:v2.0
        imagePullPolicy: IfNotPresent
        resources:
            cpu: "2"
            memory: 4Gi
        env:
            - name: PYTHONPATH
              value: /workspace

    envVars:
        LOG_LEVEL: info
        TZ: UTC

    pool:
        minIdle: 3
        maxIdle: 10

    lifecycle:
        defaultTTL: 600
        maxTTL: 7200
        idleTimeout: 300
        preStop:
            command: ["/bin/sh", "-c", "cleanup.sh"]
            timeoutSeconds: 30

    network:
        mode: block-all
        egress:
            allowedDomains:
                - "*.pypi.org"
                - "*.anaconda.org"
            allowedPorts:
                - port: 443
                  protocol: TCP
```

---

## `mainContainer`

The main sandbox container. Required.

| Field | Type | Default | Description |
|-------|------|---------|-------------|
| `image` | string | — | Container image reference. Use a public image (e.g., `python:3.12-slim`) or a private image pushed to the Sandbox0 registry. |
| `imagePullPolicy` | string | `IfNotPresent` | When to pull the image: `Always`, `IfNotPresent`, or `Never`. Use `Always` during active development to pick up the latest push. |
| `resources.cpu` | string | — | CPU limit for the sandbox (e.g., `"1"`, `"2"`, `"500m"`). |
| `resources.memory` | string | — | Memory limit for the sandbox (e.g., `"2Gi"`, `"512Mi"`). |
| `env` | array | `[]` | Per-container environment variables. Each entry has `name` and `value`. |

<Callout variant="info">
`resources.cpu` and `resources.memory` are both limits and requests — the sandbox pod is guaranteed these resources on its node.
</Callout>

---

## `envVars`

Global environment variables shared across all containers (main container and sidecars).

```yaml
envVars:
    LOG_LEVEL: info
    TZ: UTC
    APP_ENV: production
```

`envVars` are set at the template level and apply to every sandbox created from this template. Users can override or extend them at sandbox creation time via the `env_vars` field in the sandbox config.

---

## `pool`

Warm pool configuration. See [Warm Pool](/docs/template/pool) for a detailed guide.

| Field | Type | Default | Description |
|-------|------|---------|-------------|
| `minIdle` | integer | — | Minimum idle pods to pre-warm. Required. |
| `maxIdle` | integer | — | Maximum idle pods allowed. Required. |
| `autoScale` | boolean | `false` | Enable automatic pool scaling. |

---

## `lifecycle`

Default lifecycle behavior for sandboxes created from this template. All values can be overridden at sandbox creation time.

| Field | Type | Default | Description |
|-------|------|---------|-------------|
| `defaultTTL` | integer (seconds) | — | Default soft TTL. When a sandbox's TTL expires, it is automatically paused. |
| `maxTTL` | integer (seconds) | — | Maximum TTL allowed. Sandbox requests with `hard_ttl` above this value are rejected. |
| `idleTimeout` | integer (seconds) | — | Automatically pause a sandbox after it has been idle for this duration. |
| `preStop.command` | string[] | — | Shell command to run before a sandbox pod is terminated. Useful for flushing caches or saving state. |
| `preStop.timeoutSeconds` | integer | — | Maximum time to wait for the preStop command to complete. |

```yaml
lifecycle:
    defaultTTL: 600        # auto-pause after 10 min of inactivity
    maxTTL: 7200           # hard limit: 2 hours maximum
    idleTimeout: 180       # pause if no activity for 3 min
    preStop:
        command: ["/bin/sh", "-c", "/app/scripts/cleanup.sh"]
        timeoutSeconds: 30
```

---

## `network`

Default network policy applied to sandboxes created from this template. Users can override it at sandbox creation time or update it at runtime.

| Field | Type | Default | Description |
|-------|------|---------|-------------|
| `mode` | string | `allow-all` | `allow-all` permits all outbound traffic. `block-all` denies all outbound traffic except what is explicitly allowed in `egress`. |
| `egress.allowedDomains` | string[] | — | Wildcard-supported domain allowlist (e.g., `*.github.com`). Only used when `mode` is `block-all`. |
| `egress.deniedDomains` | string[] | — | Domain blocklist. Takes precedence over `allowedDomains`. |
| `egress.allowedCidrs` | string[] | — | CIDR allowlist (e.g., `10.0.0.0/8`). |
| `egress.deniedCidrs` | string[] | — | CIDR blocklist. |
| `egress.allowedPorts` | array | — | Port/protocol allowlist. Each entry: `{port: 443, protocol: TCP}`. |
| `egress.deniedPorts` | array | — | Port/protocol blocklist. |

See [Network Policy](/docs/sandbox/network) for detailed egress rule semantics and runtime update instructions.

---

## `displayName`, `description`, `tags`

Metadata fields for human-readable identification. Not used by the runtime.

| Field | Type | Description |
|-------|------|-------------|
| `displayName` | string | Short human-readable name shown in UI and `s0 template list`. |
| `description` | string | Longer description of the template's purpose. |
| `tags` | string[] | Labels for filtering and organization. |

---

## Privileged Fields

The following fields require a system-level token. They are not available to regular team API keys and are intended for platform operators configuring multi-tenant or advanced deployments.

| Field | Description |
|-------|-------------|
| `sidecars` | Additional containers running alongside the main container in the sandbox pod. Uses the standard Kubernetes container spec. |
| `pod.nodeSelector` | Pin sandbox pods to nodes matching specific labels. |
| `pod.affinity` | Node and pod affinity/anti-affinity rules. |
| `pod.tolerations` | Allow pods to be scheduled on tainted nodes. |
| `pod.serviceAccountName` | Kubernetes service account for sandbox pods. |
| `mainContainer.securityContext` | Container security context: `runAsUser`, `runAsGroup`, `capabilities.drop`. Capability `add` is not permitted. |
| `runtimeClassName` | Kubernetes runtime class (e.g., `gvisor` for gVisor isolation). |
| `clusterId` | Pin the template to a specific cluster in a multi-cluster deployment. |
| `public` | Set to `true` to make the template visible to all teams (builtin-style). |
| `allowedTeams` | Explicit list of team IDs allowed to use this template. |

<Callout variant="warning">
Attempting to set privileged fields with a regular API key returns a `403 Forbidden` error. Contact your platform administrator if you need access to these fields.
</Callout>
